questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The point of inflection for the curve $y=\frac{x+1}{x^2+1}$ is
questions.solution:
$\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-x^2-2 x+1}{\left(x^2+1\right)^2} \text { and } \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=\frac{2 x^3+6 x^2-6 x-2}{\left(x^2+1\right)^3}$
$\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}$ is 0 at three points which are the roots of the equation $x^3+3 x^2-3 x-1=0$
i.e. $-2-\sqrt{3},-2+\sqrt{3}, 1$.

Checking the sign of $\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}$ in the neighbourhood of these points, all these are the point of inflection.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $S$ be the set of real values of parameter $\lambda$ for which the equation $f(x)=2 x^3-3(2+\lambda) x^2+12 \lambda x$ has exactly one local maximum and exactly one local minimum. Then S is a subset of
questions.solution:
For one local max and $\min f^{\prime}(x)=0$ will have two real and unequal roots
$f^{\prime}(x)=6 x^2-6(2+\lambda) x+12 \lambda \Rightarrow D=(2+\lambda)^2-8 \lambda\gt0 \Rightarrow(\lambda-2)^2\gt0 \Rightarrow \lambda \in R-\{2\}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let, $f(0, \infty) \rightarrow R$ be a strictly increasing function such that $f(x)\gt-\frac{1}{x} \forall x\gt0$ and $f(x) f\left(f(x)+\frac{1}{x}\right)=1 \forall x\gt0$, then
questions.solution:
Let $f(1)=t$
Put $x=1$ in $f(x) f\left(f(x)+\frac{1}{x}\right)=1\ldots(1)$
We get
$t f(t+1)=1 \Rightarrow t \neq 0$ and $f(t+1)=\frac{1}{t}$
Put $x=t+1$ in equation (1), we get $f\left(\frac{1}{t}+\frac{1}{t+1}\right)=t=f(1)$
$\Rightarrow \frac{1}{t}+\frac{1}{t+1}=1 \Rightarrow t=\frac{1 \pm \sqrt{5}}{2}$

But for $\mathrm{t}=\frac{1+\sqrt{5}}{2}, \mathrm{t}\gt1-\mathrm{f}(1) \lt \mathrm{f}(1+\mathrm{t})=\frac{1}{\mathrm{t}} \lt 1$ a contradiction
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f$ and $g$ be functions from the interval $[0, \infty)$ to the interval $[0, \infty) f$ being an increasing function and $g$ being a decreasing function. If $f\{g(0)\}=0$, then
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { 'g' is decreasing } \\ & \Rightarrow g(1) \leq g(0) \\ & \text { Therefore } f \text { is increasing } \\ & \Rightarrow f\{g(0)\} \geq f\{g(1)\} \\ & \therefore 0 \geq f\{g(1)\} \text {, from the question. But, } f(x) \geq 0 \text {, from the question } \\ & \therefore f\{g(1)\}=0\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { As } \mathrm{x} \geq 0, \mathrm{~g}(\mathrm{x}) \leq \mathrm{g}(0) \\ & \text { So, } \mathrm{f}\{\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})\} \leq \mathrm{f}\{\mathrm{g}(0)\} \\ & \text { As } \mathrm{x} \geq 0, \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}) \geq \mathrm{f}(0) \\ & \text { So, } \mathrm{g}\{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})\} \leq \mathrm{g}\{\mathrm{f}(0)\}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}=1$ is a tangent to the curve $x=4 t, y=\frac{4}{t}, t \in R$ then
questions.solution:
Slope of tangent $=\frac{\frac{-1}{a}}{\frac{1}{b}}=-\frac{b}{a}$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\frac{d y}{d t}}{\frac{d x}{d t}}=\frac{\frac{-4}{t^2}}{4}=-\frac{1}{t^2} \lt 0 \\ & \text { So, } \frac{-b}{a} \lt 0 \Rightarrow \frac{b}{a}\gt0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Suppose that $f(x)$ is defined and positive for all real numbers $x$. If $f(x)$ increases for $x \lt 0$ and decreases for all $x\gt0$, and $g(x)=\frac{1}{f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)}$ then
options: g increases for all real x
questions.solution: $f(x)\gt0$ and $f^{\prime}(x)\gt0$ for $x \lt 0$ $f^{\prime}(x) \lt 0$ for $x\gt0$ Now $g(x)=\frac{1}{f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)}$ $g^{\prime}(x)=\frac{-1}{f^2\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)} \times f^{\prime}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) \times\left(\frac{-1}{x^2}\right)$ So for $x \lt 0 \Rightarrow g^{\prime}(x)\gt0$ $x\gt0 \Rightarrow g^{\prime}(x) \lt 0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})$ is a curve which is obtained by the reflection of $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})=\frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}-\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{x}}}{2}$ about the line $\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{x}$, then which of the following is/are true -
options: $y=g(x)$ has exactly one tangent parallel to $x$-axis
questions.solution:
Clearly $\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{f}^{-1}(\mathrm{x})=\log \left(\mathrm{x}+\sqrt{1+\mathrm{x}^2}\right)$
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & \mathrm{g}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+\mathrm{x}^2}}\gt0 \quad \forall \mathrm{x} \in \mathrm{R} \\ \because & \mathrm{g}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x}) \neq 0 \quad \forall \mathrm{x} \in \mathrm{R}\end{array}$
So no tangent is parallel to x axis.
$\Rightarrow \quad g(x)$ has no extremum.
At $(0,0) \quad g^{\prime}(0)=1$
So equation of tangent is $\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{x}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The families of curves defined by the equations $y=b x$ and $x^2+y^2=a^2$ are orthogonal if -
questions.solution:
From the first equation $\frac{d y}{d x}=b$ and from the second equation $\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{x}{y}$
$\Rightarrow \quad b\left(-\frac{x}{y}\right)=-1 \Rightarrow y=b x$
So the curves are perpendicular for every value of $\mathrm{a} \& \mathrm{~b}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=a b \sin x+b \sqrt{1-a^2} \cos x+c$, where $|a| \lt 1, b\gt0$ then
options: difference of maximum and minimum value of $f(x)$ is $2 b$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\sqrt{a^2 b^2+b^2-b^2 a^2} \sin (x+\alpha)+c=b \sin (x+\alpha)+c \text { where } \tan \alpha=\frac{b \sqrt{1-a^2}}{a b}=\frac{\sqrt{1-a^2}}{a} . \\ & f(x)_{\max }-f(x)_{\operatorname{mn}}=c+b-(c-b)=2 b \\ & \text { Also, } \alpha=\cos ^{-1} a \\ & \text { at } x=-\cos ^{-1} a \\ & f(x)=c \text {. }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f:(0, \infty) \rightarrow R$ be a differentiable function satisfying the equation $2 f(x)=f(x)+f\left(\frac{x}{y}\right) \forall x, y>0$.
If $f(1)=0$ and $f^{\prime}(1)=1$, then:
[Note: Where $[k]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $k$ ]
options: $f(x)$ has no local maxima and no local minima.
the equation $2 e \cdot f(x)=x$ has one distinct solution.
questions.solution:
Differentiate w.r.t. $y$ keeping $x$ constant
$0=x f^{\prime}(x y)-\frac{x}{y^2} f^{\prime}\left(\frac{x}{y}\right)$
$\frac{1}{y^2} f^{\prime}\left(\frac{x}{y}\right)=f^{\prime}(x y)$
Put $y=x$
$\begin{aligned} & f^{\prime}(1)=x^2 f^{\prime}\left(x^2\right)=1 \\ & f^{\prime}(t)=\frac{1}{t} \quad \forall t>0 \\ & f(x)=\ln x\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A curve passes through $(-2,-2)$ and its slope at the point $(x, y)$ is given by $\frac{1}{x \sqrt{x^2-1}}$.
Which of the following points the curve also passes through?
questions.solution:
Curve is $y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\sqrt{x^2-1}\right)-2-\frac{\pi}{3}$
Now, verify options.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)=x^2+x g^{\prime}(1)+g^{\prime \prime}(2)$ and $g(x)=f(1) x^2+x f^{\prime}(x)+f^{\prime \prime}(x)$. Then:
options: minimum value of $f(x)$ is equal to -2.25
the value of $\int_2^3 \frac{d x}{f(x)-x+5}$ is equal to $\frac{\pi}{4}$.
number of positive integral values in the domain of $\sqrt{\frac{f(x)}{g(x)}}$ is 4 .
number of points where $g(|x|)$ is non derivable is 1 .
questions.solution:
Put $a=g^{\prime}(1), b=g^{\prime \prime}(2)$
$f(x)=x^2+a x+b$                          ...(1)
$g(x)=c x^2+x(2 x+a)+2$
$\therefore \quad g(x)=(c+2) x^2+a x+2$   ...(2)
$g^{\prime}(x)=2(c+2) x+a$
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & g^{\prime}(1)=2(c+2)+a=a \\ \therefore & c=-2\end{array}$
$g^{\prime \prime}(x)=2(c+2)$
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & g^{\prime \prime}(2)=2(c+2)=b \\ \therefore & b=0\end{array}$
$\Rightarrow \quad f(x)=x^2+a x$
$g(x)=a x+2$
Now, $c=f(1)$
$\begin{array}{lll}\therefore & f(1)=1+a=c=-2 \Rightarrow a=-3 \\\Rightarrow & f(x)=x^2-3 x, & g(x)=2-3 x\end{array}$
Now, verify.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be a continuous function defined for every real $x \in R$. For any real numbers ' $a$ ' and ' $b$ ' that satisfy $a < b, f(x)$ always satisfies $f(a)>f(b)$. Then which of the followings is/are correct?
options: There is always only one real root of $f(x)=0$
There is always only one real root of $f(x)=f(-x+1)$
There is no real root of $f(x)=f(x+1)$
questions.solution:
(a) $f(x)=-(x-2)^{1 / 3} \Rightarrow \lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(2+h)-f(2)}{h}$ does not exist.
(b) $\quad f(x)=e^{-x}$
solution for $f(x)=0$ does not exist.
(c) $f(x)$ is monotonously decreasing 
and $f(-x+1)$ monotonously increases.
And since its clear that $y=f(x)$ and $y=f(-x+1)$ meets at point $\left(\frac{1}{2}, f\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)\right)$,
they must meet at only one point.
Thus, the solution for $f(x)=f(-x+1)$ is only one, $x=\frac{1}{2}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be double differentiable function such that $\left|f^{\prime \prime}(x)\right| \leq 5 \forall x \in[0,4]$ and $f$ takes its largest value at an interior point of this interval. Then the value of $\left|f^{\prime}(0)\right|+\left|f^{\prime}(4)\right|$ can be:
questions.solution:
Since, $f$ attains its maximum value at some $c \in(0,4)$
$\therefore \quad f^{\prime}(c)=0$
Now, in $y=f^{\prime}(x)$ using LMVT in $[0, c]$ and $[c, 4]$
$\left|f^{\prime \prime}(d)\right|=\left|\frac{f^{\prime}(c)-f^{\prime}(0)}{c}\right| \leq 5$
$\Rightarrow \quad\left|f^{\prime}(0)\right| \leq 5 c$   ....(1)
Again, $\quad\left|f^{\prime \prime}(d)\right|=\left|\frac{f^{\prime}(4)-f^{\prime}(c)}{4-c}\right| \leq 5$
$\Rightarrow \quad\left|f^{\prime}(4)\right| \leq 5(4-c)$ ...(2)
Eqn. (1) + eqn. (2) $\Rightarrow\left|f^{\prime}(0)\right|+\left|f^{\prime}(4)\right| \leq 20$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $d(x,[a, b])=\min .\{|x-y|: a \leq y \leq b\}$.
A function $f: R \rightarrow[0,1]$ is defined by $f(x)=\frac{d(x,[0,1])}{d(x,[0,1])+d(x,[2,3])}$, then which of the following is(are) incorrect?
options: $f(x)$ is decreasing in $(-\infty, 0)$ and increasing in $(3, \infty)$.
Number of points where $f(x)$ is non-derivable is 4 .
Number of solution of the equation $f(x)=\frac{1}{2}$ is 2 .
questions.solution: $d(x,[a, b])= \begin{cases}|x-a| & x < a \\ 0 & a \leq x \leq b \\ |x-b| & b < x\end{cases}$ $\therefore \quad d(x,[0,1])=\left[\begin{array}{ll}|x|, & x \leq 0 \\ 0, & x \in[0,1] \\ |x-1|, & x \in[1,2] \\ |x-1|, & x \in[2,3] \\ |x-1|, & x \geq 3\end{array}\right.$ $\Rightarrow \quad d(x,[2,3])=\left[\begin{array}{ll}|x-2|, & x \leq 0 \\ |x-2|, & x \in[0,1] \\ |x-2|, & x \in[1,2] \\ 0, & x \in[2,3] \\ |x-3|, & x \geq 3\end{array}\right.$ $\Rightarrow f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}\frac{|x|}{|x|+|x-2|} & x \leq 0 \\ 0 & 0 \leq x \leq 1 \\ \frac{|x-1|}{|x-1|+|x-2|} & 1 \leq x \leq 2 \\ \frac{|x-1|}{|x-1|+0}=1 & 2 \leq x \leq 3 \\ \frac{|x-1|}{|x-1|+|x-3|} & x \geq 3\end{array}=\left[\begin{array}{ll}\frac{x}{x+x-2}=\frac{x}{2(x-1)}, & x \leq 0 \\ 0, & x \in[0,1] \\ x-1, & x \in[1,2] \\ 1, & x \in[2,3] \\ \frac{x-1}{2(x-2)}, & x \geq 3\end{array}\right.\right.$ $\Rightarrow \quad 0 \leq f(x) \leq 1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f(x)$ be a monic polynomial of degree 4 satisfying the following conditions:
(i) $f^{\prime}(0)=0$
(ii) $f^{\prime}(2)=16$
(iii) for some positive real $k, f^{\prime}(x) < 0$ in the intervals $(-\infty, 0)$ and $(0, k)$. Then which of the followings is/are correct?
options: Equation $f^{\prime}(x)=0$ has one real root in the interval $(0,2)$.
If $f(0)=0$, then for all reals $x, f(x) \geq-\frac{1}{3}$.
If $f(0)=0$, then the value of $\left|\int_{-1}^1 \frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{4} d x\right|=\frac{2}{3}$.
questions.solution: Since, $f^{\prime}(0)=0$ and $f(x)$ decreases in the intervals $(-\infty, 0)$ and $(0, k)$, we infer that it contacts the line $y=f(0)$ as it passes through Then, $f(x)$ would look like the picture shown on the right. Since $f^{\prime}(2)>0$, we can notice that $k < 2$, and therefore $f^{\prime}(x)=0$ has one root in the open interval $(0,2),(a$, true) and obviously $f(x)$ does not have a local maximum ( $b$, false). If $f(0)=0$, then $f(x)=x^3(x-p)$. This leads to $f^{\prime}(x)=3 x^2(x-p)+x^3=x^2(4 x-3 p)$ and from $f^{\prime}(2)=16$, we get $p=\frac{4}{3}$. $f^{\prime}(x)=4 x^2(x-1)$, and therefore the local minimum (and thus the actual minimum of this function) occurs at $x=1$, which yields $f(1)=-\frac{1}{3},(c$, true $)$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given two function $F(x)=\left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x, G(x)=\left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{x+1}$ defined for all $x>0$. Which of the following is decreasing function for $\forall x>0$ ?
questions.solution:
$F(x)$ is an increasing function and $G(x)$ is a decreasing function
(a)$\quad F(F(x)-G(x))=\underbrace{F^{\prime}(f(x)-G(x))}_{+\mathrm{ve}} \cdot(\underbrace{F^{\prime}(x)}_{+\mathrm{ve}}-\underbrace{G^{\prime}(x)}_{-\mathrm{ve}})>0$
(b)$\quad G(F(x)-G(x))=\underbrace{G^{\prime}(F(x)-G(x))}_{-\mathrm{ve}} \cdot(\underbrace{F^{\prime}(x)}_{+\mathrm{ve}}-\underbrace{G^{\prime}(x)}_{-\mathrm{ve}}) < 0$
(c) $G^{\prime}(x)-F^{\prime}(x) < 0$
(d) $G^{\prime}(F(x)) \cdot F^{\prime}(x) < 0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f:(1, \infty) \rightarrow R$ be a differentiable function such that $\int_2^{150}(x-1) \ln (x-1)(2 f(x)-(x-1) \ln (x-1)) d x=\int_2^{150} f^2(x) d x$. Then:
options: area bounded by the curve and $x$-axis is equal to $1 / 2$.
number of solutions of the equation $f(x)=2$ is 2 .
questions.solution:
Shift LHS integral to RHS to get
$\int_2^{150}\left(f^2(x)-(x-1) \ln (x-1)(2 f(x)-(x-1) \ln (x-1))\right) d x=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad \int_2^{150}\left(f^2(x)-2 f(x)(x-1) \ln (x-1)+((x-1) \ln (x-1))^2\right) d x=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad \int_2^{150}(f(x)-(x-1) \ln (x-1))^2 d x=0$
$\begin{array}{ll}\text { But, } & (f(x)-(x-1) \ln (x-1))^2 \geq 0 \\ \text { So, } & (f(x)-(x-1) \ln (x-1))^2=0 \\ \Rightarrow & f(x)-(x-1) \ln (x-1)=0 \\ \Rightarrow & f(x)=(x-1) \ln (x-1)\end{array}$
(a) is incorrect because area is equal to $1 / 4$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f^{\prime}(x)$ be a continuous function which maps from $[0,1] \rightarrow[p(a), p(b)]$. If $p(x)$ is a differentiable function on $[a, b]$ such that $p(g(x))=x, g(0)=a$ and $g(1)=b$, then which of the following is/are true?
options: $\frac{\int_0^1 f^{\prime}(x) d x}{\int_0^1 g^{\prime}(x) d x} \leq p^{\prime}(c)$ for some $c \in(a, b)$
questions.solution:
First observe that $g$ is the inverse of $p$.
Hence $p(a)=0, p(b)=1$. Also $f^{\prime}$ is a continuous function which maps from $[0,1] \rightarrow[0,1]$
by Intermediate value theorem we can say that there is $k \in[0,1]$ such that $f^{\prime}(k)=k \Rightarrow(\mathrm{d})$
Now, $f^{\prime}(x) \leq 1 \leq(p(b)-p(a))$
Hence, $\quad \int_0^1 f^{\prime}(x) d x \leq(p(b)-p(a))$
Divide both sides by $b-a=\int_0^1 g^{\prime}(x) d x$
$\frac{\int_0^1 f^{\prime}(x) d x}{\int_0^1 g^{\prime}(x) d x} \leq \frac{(p(b)-p(a))}{b-a}=p^{\prime}(c), c \in(a, b)$ [ By LMVT] $\Rightarrow$ (c)
Also, use LMVT for $y=f(x)$ in $[0,1] \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(c)=\frac{f(1)-f(0)}{1-0} \leq 1$
$f(1)-f(0) \leq 1$
$f(1) \leq 1+f(0) \Rightarrow$(b)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider a differentiable function $f: R \rightarrow R$ with $f(0)=0$ and $f^{\prime}(0)=1$.
Which of the following statements are true for any such function $f$ ?
options: $f(x)$ is increasing on $(p, q)$ for some negative $p$ and some positive $q$.
There exists a differentiable function $g: R \rightarrow R$ such that $g^{\prime \prime}(x)=f(x)$ and
questions.solution:
Consider a differentiable function $f: R \rightarrow R$ such that $f(0)=0$ and $f^{\prime}(0)=1$.
(b) $\quad f(x)=x-2 x^2 \sin \left(\frac{1}{x}\right)$, if $x$ is distinct to 0 and $f(0)=0$.
(d) $\quad h(x)=\int_0^x f(t) d t$ fulfills $h^{\prime}(x)=f(x)$.
$g(x)=\int_0^x h(t) d t \quad \Rightarrow \quad g^{\prime}(x)=h(x)$
$\Rightarrow \quad g^{\prime \prime}(x)=h^{\prime}(x)=f(x)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=\frac{2+\ln x}{x^2}, x>0$. Identify which of the following is(are) correct about $f(x)$ ?
options: Rolle's Theorem is applicable for $f^{\prime}(x)$ in some interval of $(0, \infty)$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\frac{2+\ln x}{x^2} \\ & f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{-2 \ln x}{x^3}-\frac{3}{x^3} \\ & f^{\prime \prime}(x)=\frac{6 \ln x}{x^4}+\frac{7}{x^4}\end{aligned}$ Now, verify the options.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Function $f(x)$ is such that $f(x)=a \ln x+\frac{x^2}{2}$ where $a>0$ is a parameter. If $\frac{f\left(x_1\right)-f\left(x_2\right)}{x_1-x_2} \geq 2 \forall x_1, x_2 \in(0, \infty)$ and $x_1 \neq x_2$, then possible value of ' $a$ ' can be:
questions.solution: Solve $f^{\prime}(x) \geq 2 \forall x \in(0, \infty) \quad \Rightarrow \quad$ Range of $a$ is $[1, \infty)$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be a derivable function and $f(\alpha)=f(\beta)=0(a < \beta)$, then in the interval $(\alpha, \beta)$ :
options: $f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)=0$ has at least one real root
$f(x)-f^{\prime}(x)=0$ has at least one real root
$f(x) f^{\prime}(x)=0$ has at least one real root
$\left(f^{\prime}(x)\right)^2+f(x) f^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ has at least two real roots
questions.solution:
(a) Let $g(x)=e^x f(x)$
$\therefore g(\alpha)=g(\beta)=0$
According to Rolle's theorem, $g^{\prime}(x)=e^x f(x)+e^x f^{\prime}(x)=0$ $\Rightarrow \quad f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)=0$ will have at least one real root.
(b) Let $h(x)=e^{-x} f(x)$ similarly from Rolle's theorem $f(x)-f^{\prime}(x)$ will have at least one real root.
(c) and (d) $f^{\prime}(x)=0$ has at least one real root $\gamma \in(\alpha, \beta)$
$\therefore f(x) f^{\prime}(x)=0$ will have roots as $\alpha, \gamma, \beta$
$\therefore \quad$ Its derivative $\left(f^{\prime}(x)\right)^2+f(x) f^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ will have at least two real roots.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the greatest value of $f(x)=-x^2+4 x+\lambda-4$, where $x \in[0,5]$ is smaller than the least value of $g(x)=x^2-2 \lambda x+10-2 \lambda$, where $x \in R$ then $\lambda$ may be:
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\lambda-(x-2)^2, x \in[0,5]$
$\therefore \quad$ maximum $=\lambda$
and $g(x): \min =\lambda^2-2 \lambda^2+10-2 \lambda=10-2 \lambda-\lambda^2$
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & \lambda < 10-2 \lambda-\lambda^2 \\ \Rightarrow & \lambda^2+3 \lambda-10 < 0 \\ \therefore & \lambda \in(-5,2)\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following statement(s) is/are 'CORRECT'?
options: Rolle's theorem is applicable to the function $f(x)=1-\sqrt[5]{x^6}$ in the interval $[-1,1]$
The value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^x x e^{t^2} d t}{1+x-e^x}$ is equal to -2
If $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$ are any two unit vectors, then least value of $\frac{1}{|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|^2}+\frac{1}{|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|^2}$ is equal to 2 .
The angle between pair of tangents drawn to the curve $7 x^2-12 y^2=84$ from $M(1,2)$ is $\frac{\pi}{4}$
questions.solution:
(A) $\because f(x)=1-x^{6 / 5} \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x)=-\frac{6}{5} x^{\frac{1}{5}}$ exist $x \in(-1,1)$ and $\therefore \mathrm{f}(1)=0=\mathrm{f}(-1) \therefore$ Rolle's theorem is applicable
(B) $\because \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^x x e^{t^2} d t}{1+x-e^x}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{x\left(\int_0^x e^{t^2} d t\right)}{1+x-e^x}$
$=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{x\left(e^{x^2}\right)+\int_0^x e^{t^2} d t}{1-e^x}\left(\frac{0}{0}\right)$
$=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}=\frac{x\left(e^{x^2} \cdot 2 x\right)+e^{x^2}+e^{x^2}}{-e^x}=\frac{0+1+1}{-1}=-2$
(C) $\because|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=1+1+2 \cos \theta=2(1+\cos \theta)=4 \cos ^2 \theta / 2$
$\begin{aligned}
& \quad \text { and }|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|^2=1+1-2 \cos \theta=2(1-\cos \theta)=4 \sin ^2 \theta / 2 \\
& \therefore \frac{1}{|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|^2}+\frac{1}{|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|^2} \\
& =\frac{1}{4}\left(\sec ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}+\operatorname{cosec}^2 \frac{\theta}{2}\right) \\
& = \\
& =\frac{1}{4}\left(1+\tan ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}+1+\cot ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}\right)\qquad \because A.M. \geq G.M \\
& \quad \therefore \tan ^2 \frac{\theta}{2} \cot ^2 \frac{\theta}{2} \geq 2 \geq 1
\end{aligned}$
(D) $\because M(1,2)$ lies on the director circle of $7 x^2-12 y^2=84$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f:\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right] \rightarrow[0,1]$ be a differentiable function such that $f(0)=0, f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=1$, then
questions.solution:
(A) Consider $g(x)=\sin ^{-1} f(x)-x$
since $g(0)=0, g\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=0$
$\therefore \quad$ there is at least one value of $\alpha \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$ such that $\mathrm{g}^{\prime}(\alpha)$
$=\frac{f^{\prime}(\alpha)}{\sqrt{1-(f(\alpha))^2}}-1=0$
i.e. $\quad f^{\prime}(\alpha)=\sqrt{1-(f(\alpha))^2}$ for atleast one value of $\alpha$ but may
not be for all $\alpha \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \quad \therefore \quad$ false
(B) Consider $g(x)=f(x)-\frac{2 x}{\pi}$
$\text { since } g(0)=0, g\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=0$
$\therefore \quad$ there is at least one value of $\alpha \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$ such that $\mathrm{g}^{\prime}(\alpha)$
$=f^{\prime}(\alpha)-\frac{2}{\pi}=0$
i.e. $\quad f^{\prime}(\alpha)=\frac{2}{\pi}$ for atleast one value of $\alpha$ but may not be for all
$\begin{aligned}
& \alpha \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \\
& \therefore \quad \text { false }
\end{aligned}$
(C) Consider $\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})=(\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}))^2-\frac{2 \mathrm{x}}{\pi}$
$\text { since } g(0)=0, g\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=0$
$\therefore \quad$ there is at least one value of $\alpha \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$ such that $\mathrm{g}^{\prime}(\alpha)$
$=2 f(\alpha) f^{\prime}(\alpha)-\frac{2}{\pi}=0$
$\therefore \quad f(\alpha) f^{\prime}(\alpha)=\frac{1}{\pi}$
$\therefore \quad$ true
(D) Consider $g(x)=f(x)-\frac{4 x^2}{\pi^2}$
$\text { since } g(0)=0, g\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=0$
$\therefore \quad$ there is at least one value of $\alpha \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$ such
$\text { that } g^{\prime}(\alpha)=f^{\prime}(\alpha)-\frac{8 \alpha}{\pi^2}=0$
$\therefore \quad f^{\prime}(\alpha)=\frac{8 \alpha}{\pi^2} \quad \therefore \quad \text { true }$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$P(x)$ is a fourth degree polynomial such that
(1) $P(-x)=P(x) \forall x \in R$,
(2) $P(x)\gt0 \forall x \in R$,
(3) $P(0)=1$,
(4) $P(x)$ has exactly two local minima at $x_1$ and $x_2$ such that $\left|x_1-x_2\right|=2$.

The line $y=1$ touches the curve at a certain point $Q$ and the enclosed area between the line and the curve is $\frac{8 \sqrt{2}}{15}$. Let $g(x)=A x^2+B x+C(A \neq 0)$ such that $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{P(x)-g(x)-g(-x)}{x^2}$ is finite and is equal to the slope of the tangent of $g(x)$ at $x=-1$. Also, $P(x)$ and $g(x)$ have common tangent at $Q$ parallel to $x$-axis.
questions.solution:
$P(x)$ is an even function.
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{ax}^4+b \mathrm{x}^2+1$ and $\mathrm{P}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=4 \mathrm{ax}^3+2 \mathrm{bx}=2 \mathrm{x}\left(2 \mathrm{ax}^2+\mathrm{b}\right)$
It has two minima. Hence, $\mathrm{a}\gt$ and $\mathrm{b} \lt 0$.
So, at $x= \pm \sqrt{\frac{-\mathrm{b}}{2 \mathrm{a}}}, \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{x})$ has minima.
$2 \sqrt{\frac{-b}{2 a}}=2 \Rightarrow b=-2 a$
$\therefore$ Maximum at $(0,1)$.
Also, $\frac{8 \sqrt{2}}{15}=2 \int_0^{\sqrt{\frac{-b}{a}}}\left(1-\left(a x^4+\mathrm{bx}^2+1\right)\right) \mathrm{dx} \Rightarrow \mathrm{b}=-1 \mathrm{a}=\frac{1}{2}$
Now, $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{P(x)-(g(x)+g(-x))}{x^2}$ is finite.
$\Rightarrow C=\frac{1}{2}, B=-1$
Also, $y=1$ is tangent to $A x^2-x+\frac{1}{2}=f(x)$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{Ax}-\mathrm{x}+\frac{1}{2}=1$ has equal roots
$\Rightarrow A=-\frac{1}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be a polynomial function satisfying $0 < x f(y) < y f(x) \forall x, y$ such that $0 < x < y < 1$ and $f(0)=0$ then:
questions.solution:
$x f(y) < y f(x) \forall 0 < x < y < 1$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{f(y)}{y} < \frac{f(x)}{x} \forall x < y \\ & \Rightarrow \quad g(x)=\frac{f(x)}{x} \text { is decreasing } \\ & \Rightarrow \quad g^{\prime}(x) < 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x f^{\prime}(x) < f(x) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad f^{\prime}(x) < \frac{f(x)}{x} \forall x 0 < x < 1\end{aligned}$
Hence, $\quad f^{\prime}(x) < g(x) \forall x 0 < x < 1$
Hence, $f^{\prime}(x)$ is less than minimum value of $g(x)$ which is $g(1)$.
$\therefore \quad f^{\prime}(x) < f(1)$        ...(1)
$x f^{\prime}(x) < f(x) \quad \Rightarrow \quad \int_0^1 x f^{\prime}(x) d x < \int_0^1 f(x) d x$
$\left.\Rightarrow \quad x f(x)\right|_0 ^1-\int_0^1 f(x) d x < \int_0^1 f(x) d x$
$\Rightarrow \quad f(1) < 2 \int_0^1 f(x) d x$ ...(2)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(\mathrm{x})=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}|\mathrm{x}-2|+\mathrm{k}, & \mathrm{x} \leq 2 \\ 3 \mathrm{x}+2, & \mathrm{x}\gt2\end{array}\right.$. If $f(\mathrm{x})$ has a local minimum at $\mathrm{x}=2$, then k can be equal to -
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}2-x+k, & x \leq 2 \\ 3 x+2, & x\gt2\end{array}\right.$
$\because y=2-x+k$ is a decreasing function \& $\mathrm{y}=3 \mathrm{x}+2$ is an increasing function, $f(\mathrm{x})$ will have a local minimum at $x=2$ if $f(2) \leq f(x)$ for $x\gt2$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & \mathrm{k} \leq 3 \mathrm{x}+2 \text { for } \mathrm{x}\gt2 \\ \Rightarrow & \mathrm{k} \leq 8\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If $g(x)=7 x^2 e^{-x} \quad \forall x \in R$, then $g(x)$ has -
options: two local maxima and one local minima
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & g^{\prime}(x)=14 x e^{-x^2}-14 x^3 e^{-x^2} \\ & =14 x e^{-x^2}\left(1-x^2\right)=-14 x(x+1)(x-1) e^{-x^2}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
